quoteLast: , they proceeded, at a rapid pace and by the most direct route, to Mundell’s Krantz, descended by the road made obliquely down the face of the krantz by Captain Somerset’s company, and reached their home at Brown’s farm in the afternoon.
title: The History of the Rifle Brigade (the Prince Consort's Own) Formerly the 95th

quoteFirst: The order of the day was as follows: We rose
quoteLast: and took a bath in Eden Lake, where several bathing houses had been constructed. The washing and repairing of clothes was attended to--under the superintendence of a member who was an expert in such matters--by a band of Swahili, to whom this work was allotted as their sole duty.
title: Freeland: A Social Anticipation
author: Hertzka, Theodor, 1845-1924; Ransom, Arthur, 1831-1912 [Translator]

quoteLast: when Susi heard Majwara's step once more. "Come to Bwana, I am afraid; I don't know if he is alive." The lad's evident alarm made Susi run to arouse Chumah, Chowperé, Matthew, and Muanyaséré, and the six men went immediately to the hut.

quoteFirst: The commander-in-chief then issued an order to all his ships, prescribing the entire details of the surrender. The American battle squadron got under way
quoteLast: November 21, 1918, and steamed from the Forth bridge out of the Firth into the North Sea.
title: Our Navy in the War
author: Perry, Lawrence, 1875-1954

quoteFirst: It was Sunday night when we saw the enemy. We were ready for action, but were lying down to have a rest, when orders came to stand at our posts. It was
quoteLast: on Monday when we started to fire; we were at it all day till six p.m., when we started to advance. Then the bugle sounded the charge, and the cavalry and infantry charged like madmen at the enemy; then the enemy fell back about forty miles, so we held them at bay till Wednesday, when the enemy was reinforced.
title: In the Firing Line: Stories of the War by Land and Sea
author: Adcock, Arthur St. John, 1864-1930
